testcase: primaryscan3 operator, catch limit in fetch
statements: SELECT d.a FROM customer d LET v = (SELECT d1.a FROM customer AS d1 WHERE d.a = d1.a);
error: Correlated subquery's keyspace (d1) cannot have more than 1000 documents without appropriate secondary index

testcase: indexscan3 operator, catch limit in indexscan3
statements: SELECT d.a FROM customer d LET v = (SELECT meta(d1).id FROM customer AS d1 WHERE d.a = 1);
error: Correlated subquery's keyspace (d1) cannot have more than 1000 documents without appropriate secondary index

statements: SELECT product.id as id, product.name as name, ( SELECT customer.id AS id, customer.name AS name FROM shellTest customer USE KEYS ( SELECT RAW `to` FROM shellTest WHERE `from`=product.id)) AS customers FROM shellTest product WHERE product.id IN [ "1", "2"]
SELECT month FROM [{"month":1}] AS s
SELECT month FROM (SELECT month FROM [{"month":1}] AS s) AS sub
select a, (SELECT RAW (SELECT RAW a) ) AS b  from [1,2,3] a

testcase: MB-60011, part 1, covering index scan on primary index
statements: SELECT (SELECT 1 AS one FROM shellTest AS d WHERE META(d).id = t.`to`) AS sub, t.`from`, t.`to`, META(t).id FROM shellTest t WHERE t.`from` = '2'

testcase: MB-60011, part 2, non-covering index scan on primary index
statements: SELECT (SELECT d.id FROM shellTest AS d WHERE META(d).id = t.`to`) AS sub, t.`from`, t.`to`, META(t).id FROM shellTest t WHERE t.`from` = '2'

SELECT u.qty, COUNT(1) AS cnt FROM (SELECT ARRAY {'qty': v.qty} FOR v IN orderlines WHEN v.price > 2.0 END AS quantities FROM orders AS t WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' AND META().id LIKE 'subqexp_%' ) AS d UNNEST d.quantities AS u GROUP BY u.qty UNION SELECT u.qty, COUNT(1) AS cnt FROM (SELECT ARRAY {'qty': v.qty} FOR v IN orderlines WHEN v.price > 2.0 END AS quantities FROM orders AS t WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' AND META().id LIKE 'subqexp_%' ) AS d UNNEST d.quantities AS u GROUP BY u.qty

SELECT a   FROM ['abc', 1, 2.5 ] AS a ORDER BY a

SELECT a.x FROM [{'x':11},{'x':12},'abc'] AS a ORDER BY a.x

SELECT r   FROM {'p':[{'x':11},{'x':12}],'q':'abc','r':null}.r

SELECT id, (SELECT RAW SUM(orderlines.price) FROM orders.orderlines)[0] AS total FROM orders WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
SELECT id, (SELECT orderlines FROM orders.orderlines ORDER BY orderlines.price) orderlines FROM orders WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
SELECT id, (SELECT orderlines FROM orders.orderlines ORDER BY orderlines.price DESC) orderlines FROM orders WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
SELECT id FROM orders WHERE EXISTS (SELECT raw 1 FROM orders.orderlines WHERE orderlines.qty >= 1) AND test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
SELECT id FROM orders WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT raw 1 FROM orders.orderlines WHERE orderlines.qty > 1) AND test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
SELECT id FROM orders WHERE 'lunch' IN (SELECT RAW orderlines.productId FROM orders.orderlines) AND test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
SELECT id FROM orders WHERE 'drink' IN (SELECT RAW orderlines.productId FROM orders.orderlines) AND test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
SELECT id, total FROM orders LET total = (SELECT RAW SUM(orderlines.price) FROM orders.orderlines)[0] WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
SELECT id, total FROM orders WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' GROUP by id LETTING total = (SELECT RAW SUM(orderlines.price) FROM orders.orderlines)[0] ORDER BY id
error: Expression (correlated (select raw sum((`orderlines`.`price`)) from (`orders`.`orderlines`))[0]) (near line 1, column 84) must depend only on group keys or aggregates.

statements: SELECT id, (SELECT RAW SUM(item.price) FROM items as item)[0] total FROM orders LET items = (SELECT orderlines.* FROM orders.orderlines ORDER BY orderlines.price) WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' ORDER BY id
MERGE INTO orders USING orders o USE KEYS VALIDATE ['subqexp_1235', 'subqexp_1236', 'bad_key'] ON KEY id WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT {o.id,'test_id':'subqexp'}
SELECT id FROM orders USE KEYS ['1235', '1236'] ORDER BY id
DELETE FROM orders USE KEYS VALIDATE ['1235', '1236', 'bad_key']

comments: Repeat without VALIDATE and there should be no warning
statements: DELETE FROM orders USE KEYS ['1235', '1236', 'bad_key']
MERGE INTO orders USING (SELECT 's'||id  AS id FROM orders WHERE test_id = 'subqexp' ) o ON KEY o.id WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT {o.id,'test_id':'subqexp'}
SELECT id FROM orders USE KEYS ['s1235', 's1236'] ORDER BY id
DELETE FROM orders USE KEYS ['s1235', 's1236']

MERGE INTO orders USING (SELECT 'se'||id  AS id, (SELECT RAW SUM(orderlines.price) FROM orders.orderlines)[0] AS total FROM orders WHERE test_id = 'subqexp') o ON KEY o.id WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT {o.id, o.total, 'test_id':'subqexp'}
SELECT id, total FROM orders USE KEYS ['se1235', 'se1236'] ORDER BY id
DELETE FROM orders USE KEYS ['se1235', 'se1236']
MERGE INTO orders USING [{'id':'c1235'},{'id':'c1236'}] o ON KEY id WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT {o.id, 'test_id':'subqexp'}
SELECT id FROM orders USE KEYS ['c1235', 'c1236'] ORDER BY id
